steps.title: Use a Desmos shortcut with a convenient value of s
steps.content: Because $s$ is a constant and the answer choices all use $s$, choose an easy value such as $s=2$ to make the table numeric. Then the table points become $(-4,28)$, $(2,13)$, and $(-2,23)$. Enter $x_1=[-4,2,-2]$ and $y_1=[28,13,23]$, then type $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=-\frac{5}{2}$ and $b=18$, so $y=-\frac{5}{2}x+18$.

steps.title: Translate the numeric result back to s
steps.content: With $s=2$, the Desmos line $y=-\frac{5}{2}x+18$ becomes $2y=-5x+36$, or $5x+2y=36$. Replacing the chosen value $2$ with the general constant $s$ gives $5x+sy=18s$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $sx+5y=18s$: plug in $(s,13)$ → $s^2+65$, not $18s$ for general $s$ — fails.
  • B) $5x+sy=18s$: all three table points satisfy it (verified above).
  • C) $5x+sy=18$: plug $(s,13)$ → $5s+13s=18s\ne 18$ unless $s=1$ — fails.
  • D) $sx+5y=18$: plug $(s,13)$ → $s^2+65\ne 18$ — fails.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
